user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: .codex_history/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp.codexhist.json
## My request for Codex:
Excuse the interruption, your thoughts pointed out a potential friction point I want to resolve in a particular way, about the samples-per-pass

For context, in another session, we have started to add MP3 compression, to reduce file sizes and speed up download speeds. But the encoder `arduino-liblame` is a bit too slow, and we have some FIFO overflows during recording, the recordings are not completely unusable, but we are getting around 160 FIFO overflows in a 30 second recording while using bluetooth. So I want to explorer using a fixed point encoder to improve the performance just slightly, instead of more drastic actions like using mono instead of stereo. I have put `shine` encoder in the project to accomplish this. Read `.codex_history\lib\AudioFileRecorder\AudioFileRecorder.cpp.codexhist.json` for more context about what was recently changed and why.
Implement `lib\ShineWrapper\ShineWrapper.cpp` and `lib\ShineWrapper\ShineWrapper.h`
It is to be a class object, very similar to `MP3EncoderLAME`
The constructor will take in parameters:
- uint32_t sample_rate
- int num_of_channels
- int bits_per_channel
- int quality
- int bit_rate kbps
- bool disable_bit_reservoir
- callback with shape `typedef void (*MP3CallbackFDK)(uint8_t *mp3_data, size_t len);`
And then instantiate a copy of `shine_config_t` with those settings internally and store it
A `begin()` function will call `shine_initialise`, which will allocate and return a `shine_global_config`, and we store it
A function `getSamplesPerPass` that simply wraps `shine_samples_per_pass`
When `begin()` is called, get the data for `shine_samples_per_pass` and allocate internal buffers accordingly

A function `int32_t write(void *pcm_samples, int bytes)` , which uses the internal buffers, if we have buffered enough data, internally call `shine_encode_buffer_interleaved` and then if data is returned, call the callback function with data
Skip the buffering if the code realizes that the input size matches the input buffer size (and the buffer is empty). The point is to avoid just one more for-loop.
A function `flush()` that calls `shine_flush` and then immediately call the callback function with data
An `end()` function, clean up any allocated buffers, calls `shine_close`, and also, the destructor calls `end()` if it hasn't been called
Add a `library.json` file that sets up the compilation of this `ShineWrapper` library correctly, the `lib\ShineWrapper\shine\src\bin` is to be completely ignored
There is zero dependency on `arduino-liblame` or any of its contents

For all the stuff in `AudioFileRecorder` that uses `arduino-liblame`, put it all inside blocks activated by `BUILD_MP3_USE_LIBLAME` , and then using `BUILD_MP3_USE_SHINE`, implement the same operations using `ShineWrapper`.

You will need to think about if extra steps are needed to change FIFO dequeuing sizes, as I think by internally buffering again, we might not need to, but maybe there’s performance gains if we actually did not internally buffer.

When the file is finished recording, the code path for `liblame` is a bit complicated as I didn’t understand how to safely flush, but `shine` has a `flush` so use it instead of stuffing zeros until it works
There might be more constants needed in `inc/conf.h` or maybe some of them with `_LAME_` inside might get renamed to be more generic.
agent_reply: - Split `AudioFileRecorder` MP3 encoder code between `BUILD_MP3_USE_LIBLAME` and `BUILD_MP3_USE_SHINE`.
- Shine now uses `getSamplesPerPass()` after `begin()`, and normal recorder pumping waits for a full encoder pass so the wrapper usually takes the no-copy fast path.
- Shine stop/close uses `flush()` directly; the LAME silence-padding workaround is now LAME-only.
- Renamed MP3 constants in [conf.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h) to generic names and removed the always-on arduino-liblame include paths/dependency.
Note: the worktree already has unrelated dirty Shine bin files and `src/version.c`; I left those alone.

- Shine now uses `getSamplesPerPass()` after `begin()`, and normal recorder pumping waits for a full encoder pass so the wrapper usually takes the no-copy fast path.
- Shine stop/close uses `flush()` directly; the LAME silence-padding workaround is now LAME-only.
- Renamed MP3 constants in [conf.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h) to generic names and removed the always-on arduino-liblame include paths/dependency.
